body.title: agent_datacenter: device registration hard-fails on namespace collision
body.result: Skeleton.register_device() now checks registry status: raises RegistrationError if online/blocked, allows reattach if offline. RegistrationError.docstring updated.
body.description:
Namespace collision at registration is a hard fail — rejected immediately, before the device starts. Discovering a collision at call time means a live system is in an inconsistent state.

Proposed shape:
- In Skeleton.register_device(): before registering, check if device_id already in registry
  If exists and status != 'offline': raise RegistrationError(f'Device {device_id} already registered and online')
  If exists and status == 'offline': allow re-registration (device reattach scenario)
- RegistrationError logged with device_id, timestamp, and existing device's status
- The calling device does not start; error surfaced to caller immediately
- rack.devices() shows no entry for the failed device

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/skeleton/skeleton.py (Skeleton.register_device)
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/skeleton/exceptions.py (RegistrationError)

Design rules:
- No design rules flag for this — pure infrastructure invariant
- docs-in-code: RegistrationError docstring explains the rationale (fail at registration, not at call time)

Scope boundary:
IN: hard-fail logic, RegistrationError exception class
OUT: any retry or conflict-resolution logic — rejection is final

Test plan:
- Register device 'igor'; register 'igor' again → RegistrationError raised
- Register device 'igor'; mark it offline; register 'igor' again → success (reattach)
- After failed registration: rack.devices() does not contain a duplicate
